name: Rozhanitsy
description: The Rozhanitsy are a pair of ancient Slavic deities, often referred to as Goddesses of Fate and Family. They are typically depicted as two women, sometimes accompanied by a third figure called Sudenitsy, who determine the destiny of newborn children and influence the well-being of families. Their origins are shrouded in the mists of pre-Christian Slavic beliefs, suggesting a deep connection to the earth, fertility, and the continuation of life. Rozhanitsy are believed to possess the power to influence the length and quality of a person's life, shaping their fortune and misfortunes. They are invoked during childbirth and other important family events to ensure prosperity, health, and happiness. Offerings of food, such as cheese, honey, and porridge, were traditionally made to appease them and secure their blessings. While their worship diminished with the arrival of Christianity, remnants of their veneration can still be found in folk customs and beliefs surrounding childbirth and family life, highlighting their enduring significance in Slavic culture as protectors of the home, childbearing, and the overall well-being of future generations. They embody the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth, representing the interconnectedness of family and the enduring power of fate.
subtitle: Goddesses of Fate and Family

keyMyths.title: The Naming Ceremony
keyMyths.description: The Rozhanitsy appear three days after a child's birth to whisper the child's fate. Offerings are made to ensure a favorable destiny.

keyMyths.title: Protecting the Home
keyMyths.description: The Rozhanitsy safeguard the family hearth, warding off evil spirits and ensuring the prosperity of the household. They are honored with rituals and offerings.

keyMyths.title: Weaving Destiny
keyMyths.description: The Rozhanitsy weave the threads of fate, connecting individuals to their past, present, and future. Their actions influence the course of lives.
